_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description:
You can:
- review the organisation’s roles, responsibilities, policies, standards and attitudes to data
- decide the work needed to improve data governance, taking into account effort, reward and risk
- guide decision making on the most appropriate methods of implementing governance
- put in place data governance processes that ensure compliance and minimise risk
Examples of leadership using this skill:
- working with senior stakeholders to agree the level of risk the organisation is willing to tolerate
- getting senior stakeholders to prioritise improving data governance despite competing or changing priorities
- building stakeholders' confidence in new owners of data assets, for example, through training plans
reference_document_element_description: Skill in building and managing a Data Governance Council, including membership design, decision authority, meeting cadence, escalation processes, and conflict resolution.
comments: DDaT skill equal WIDAI DG domain. DG-S-004 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DG-S-005 (DG, cosine: 0.733), DG-A-002 (DG, cosine: 0.722).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.9/10 (raw 0.487). Shared vocabulary: [building, data, decision, governance, processes, skill]. DDaT-only terms [account, agree, appropriate, assets, attitudes, changing] vs WIDAI-only terms [authority, cadence, conflict, council, design, escalation] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.839 (P=0.818, R=0.862). R exceeds P by 0.045 — the WIDAI KSA's 21 words are well-captured by the DDaT element's 107 words, but the DDaT element contains broader scope not fully reflected in the matched WIDAI KSA.

nli_cross_encoder.significance:
NLI: neutral-dominant (99.2%). "You can:
- review the organisation’s roles, responsibilities, policies, standard" and "Skill in building and managing a Data Governance Council, including membership d" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.724 > STS 0.487 (gap +0.237). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [building, data, decision, governance, processes] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 9.3%. Shared tokens: [building, data, decision, governance, processes, skill]. DDaT-only: [account, agree, appropriate, assets, attitudes, changing]. WIDAI-only: [authority, cadence, conflict, council, design, escalation].

signals.detail: STS 0.487 barely clears classification threshold for Equal. This match is fragile and may indicate insufficient KSA coverage for this competency area.

Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.237 with low STS 0.487. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
